name: proxpi
perf_notes: Lightweight, designed to cut PyPI load and speed installs in CI. In-memory index cache (30-min default TTL) plus a disk-backed LRU package-file cache (5 GB default). Default Docker entrypoint runs Gunicorn with 2 threads (single process); cache is thread-safe but not shared across processes. On slow upstream downloads it can redirect the client to upstream (PROXPI_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT, default 0.9s) rather than block. No published throughput/latency benchmarks.
scale_notes: Single-process design: the index/file cache lives in-process and is explicitly "not synchronised across multiple processes" - the README recommends scaling with threads, not processes/replicas. No HA/clustering, no shared-cache coordination, no leader election. Multiple independent instances can run behind a load balancer but each maintains its own cache.
summary: A lightweight Flask/Gunicorn pull-through PyPI caching proxy that lazily mirrors the simple index and package files (merging a primary plus extra upstream indices) for CI and local networks, with no auth, database, or private-package hosting.
extras: Merges a primary index (PROXPI_INDEX_URL) with multiple extra upstream indices (PROXPI_EXTRA_INDEX_URLS) into one served /index/ endpoint
Per-index configurable cache TTLs (PROXPI_INDEX_TTL default 30m; extra-index TTL PROXPI_EXTRA_INDEX_TTLS default 3m)
Cache invalidation via a tiny web page at / plus DELETE /cache/<project> and DELETE /cache/list endpoints
Exposes a GET /health endpoint (added in v1.2) for liveness checks

Acts as both PEP 658/714 metadata producer and consumer and preserves data-yanked, hashes and requires-python when re-rendering; PEP 700 project versions, file size and upload-time (simple repository API v1.1) are implemented on master but still listed under Unreleased, so not yet in a tagged release (latest 1.3.0, 2026-05-12)

client_uv is partial because proxpi's released 1.3.0 does not yet serve PEP 700 upload-time: ordinary uv installs/resolution work fine over the PEP 503/691 simple index, but uv's --exclude-newer cooldown flag treats distributions without an upload-time field as unavailable, so that uv-specific feature is broken through proxpi until PEP 700 ships
Falls back to redirecting clients to upstream when a download exceeds PROXPI_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT (default 0.9s)
Disk file cache is size-bounded LRU (PROXPI_CACHE_SIZE, default 5GB) with atomic/crash-safe downloads (atomic file downloads added in v1.3.0)

Optional 'pretty' extra (coloredlogs + colored-traceback) for coloured logging/tracebacks (disable colour with NO_COLOR=1); logging level via PROXPI_LOGGING_LEVEL (added v1.3.0). Logs are human-readable coloured text, not structured/JSON
Optional PROXPI_DISABLE_INDEX_SSL_VERIFICATION and PROXPI_BINARY_FILE_MIME_TYPE knobs; documented GitLab CI / GitHub Actions usage
No authentication of any kind: everything is anonymous read; no upload API, no private-package hosting, no search endpoint
Official Docker image epicwink/proxpi (ARM + x86); runs anywhere Python/Gunicorn run, no database. HTTPS only via flask run cert options or a fronting reverse proxy
